summary: Downscale a video to a target height, preserving aspect, and re-encode at a named profile.

parity.note: Deterministic for a fixed ffmpeg build: libx264 at a pinned preset/crf over identical input bytes emits identical output bytes. The condition is the BUILD, not the box — local and pod run the same pinned ffmpeg, and tests/test_op_media_scale.py asserts the argv is a pure function of params so a drift shows as an argv diff rather than as mystery-different pixels. Across ffmpeg majors this degrades to perceptual; that is a version-pin problem and belongs to the image, not to this op.

height.description: Target height in pixels. Width follows from the source aspect (-2 = nearest even). The DECISION is 'how big'; how it is scaled is the handler's.

encode_profile.description: Named quality tier, NOT encoder flags. `-preset veryfast -crf 26` is tuning and stays in the handler (scripts/render_profile.py is the same SSOT pattern). Sending argv here would publish the encoder ladder and void additionalProperties:false.
